pitch_text: In the City of Joy for the first time in this edition, it is the iconic Eden Gardens. We are on pitch number 5, the exact same pitch which was used in that game - South Africa v New Zealand, where Finn Allen smashed his way to a 33-ball hundred. It was a pretty good one, the game finished within 13 overs. The dimensions first, I am at the far end, to my right is 61 meters, to my left is 68 meters, a hit down the ground is 74 meters, so the longest hit around here is 74. That pitch was good, what about this one? It is a little bit sticky (the weather), sticky might help the swing bowlers because they gonna need something on this. It needs to swing, it needs to do something, looks like an absolute belter. Only little areas of concern - couple of slightly bare patches in and around, I don't think they will have much effect whatsoever with the ball coming onto the bat. Last year 199 was the average score batting first throughout the tournament, good luck to the bowlers cause they are going to need it. Expect fours and sixes all around this beautiful ground. Chasing, sticky nature of tonight, chasing will be the way to go. Chase and win even if it is more than 200, inform Murali Karthik and Simon Doull.
notes: Match 6 IPL 2026: KKR chose to bowl → SRH bat first. Impact pools: SRH Harshal Patel, Liam Livingstone, Eshan Malinga, R Smaran, Sakib Hussain; KKR Finn Allen, Manish Pandey, Saurabh Dubey, Rovman Powell, Tejasvi Dahiya. Process 1A uses Harshal Patel + Finn Allen as 12th. Varun Chakravarthy spelling per ipl_2026_squads (not Chakaravarthy).
